summary.note: change_fraction_vs_full is the proportion of the 200 samples where removing the named component changes the dominant emotion label. It is a structural measure of the component's influence on the taxonomy; labelled emotion ground truth was not available, so this is not an accuracy ablation.

per_sample.text: Apple reported record quarterly revenue of $124 billion, beating analyst expectations by a wide margin.
NVIDIA shares surged 12% after the company raised its full-year guidance significantly.
Tesla delivered 500,000 vehicles in Q4, setting a new all-time delivery record.
Amazon Web Services revenue grew 37% year-over-year, accelerating from last quarter.
Microsoft earnings crushed estimates with cloud revenue up 46% and strong margins.
The Federal Reserve signaled it may cut interest rates sooner than expected, boosting markets.
Goldman Sachs upgraded the stock to buy with a price target of $250, citing strong fundamentals.
Pfizer's new drug received FDA approval, opening a $10 billion market opportunity.
The company announced a $50 billion share buyback program and increased its dividend by 15%.
Retail sales jumped 1.7% in December, well above the 0.4% economists had forecast.
Netflix added 13 million subscribers in Q4, far exceeding the 8.7 million expected.
The S&P 500 hit a new all-time high as tech stocks rallied on strong earnings.
JPMorgan posted record annual profit of $49.6 billion, driven by higher interest rates.
AMD gained market share from Intel in both server and desktop processor segments.
The unemployment rate fell to 3.4%, the lowest level in 53 years.
Shopify beat revenue estimates and reported its first full year of profitability.
Boeing received a massive order for 200 aircraft from IndiGo, worth $26 billion.
Strong consumer spending drove GDP growth to 3.3%, above the 2.0% consensus.
Google's advertising revenue rebounded strongly, up 11% year-over-year.
The company's profit margins expanded to 28%, the highest in five years.
Visa reported 10% growth in cross-border transaction volumes, signaling strong travel demand.
CrowdStrike raised its annual revenue forecast for the third consecutive quarter.
Eli Lilly's weight loss drug Mounjaro showed remarkable results in clinical trials.
The housing market showed signs of recovery with permits rising 7% month over month.
Meta's Reality Labs losses narrowed while ad revenue growth accelerated to 25%.
Costco reported same-store sales growth of 9.1%, well ahead of expectations.
The company successfully refinanced its debt at lower rates, saving $200M annually.
Palantir won a major $480M government contract, its largest deal ever.
Broadcom completed its $69 billion acquisition of VMware, creating a software giant.
The stock rallied 8% on news of a breakthrough partnership with a major tech firm.
Walmart's e-commerce sales surged 24%, driving total revenue ahead of estimates.
The company's free cash flow improved 40% year-over-year, enabling debt reduction.
Caterpillar reported record revenue as infrastructure spending boosted equipment demand.
Disney+ gained 7 million subscribers and moved closer to profitability.
The biotech company announced positive Phase 3 trial results for its cancer treatment.
Morgan Stanley beat earnings expectations with strong wealth management revenue.
Home Depot's comparable sales rose for the first time in a year, surprising analysts.
The Fed's preferred inflation gauge fell to 2.6%, moving closer to the 2% target.
UnitedHealth raised its 2025 earnings guidance, citing strong enrollment growth.
Salesforce reported record operating margins of 33% as efficiency initiatives paid off.
The company's new product launch exceeded expectations with $1B in first-week sales.
TSMC reported record quarterly revenue driven by AI chip demand.
Starbucks same-store sales turned positive in its key China market.
The manufacturing PMI expanded for the first time in 16 months, signaling recovery.
Qualcomm signed a long-term chip supply deal with Samsung worth billions.
The company announced a strategic review that could unlock significant shareholder value.
PayPal's transaction volume grew 15% and active accounts increased sequentially.
Oracle's cloud infrastructure revenue doubled year-over-year to $1.8 billion.
Investors cheered the strong jobs report, sending the Dow up 400 points.
The company's backlog reached an all-time high of $170 billion.
ServiceNow raised its subscription revenue guidance after a strong Q3.
Block reported record gross profit growth of 22% across its Cash App platform.
Uber achieved profitability for the full year for the first time in its history.
Lululemon beat expectations with strong international growth of 48% year-over-year.
The IPO priced above its expected range, reflecting strong investor demand.
Chevron announced a $75 billion buyback, the largest in the oil industry's history.
Datadog's annual recurring revenue crossed $2 billion ahead of schedule.
Consumer confidence surged to its highest level in two years.
The company's AI product saw 300% growth in enterprise adoption this quarter.
Moderna received emergency approval for its next-generation vaccine in 30 countries.
The merger is expected to create $3 billion in annual cost synergies.
Deere & Company beat earnings by 20% on strong agricultural equipment demand.
The company's debt-to-equity ratio improved to 0.3, the healthiest in its sector.
Snap's daily active users grew 10% to 414 million, a new record.
Adobe reported record digital media revenue driven by AI-powered creative tools.
The semiconductor index hit a 52-week high on AI infrastructure spending optimism.
Rivian beat delivery expectations and narrowed its net loss significantly.
The company guided for 25% revenue growth next year, above consensus of 18%.
ConocoPhillips raised its dividend for the 8th consecutive year.
Airbnb reported record nights booked and expanded its average daily rate.

China's real estate crisis deepened as Evergrande filed for bankruptcy protection.
